subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, World Bank, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Brazilian official (IBGE, Banco Central, Tesouro) + Lusophone/regional press.
NEGATIVE-SKEW case: the fiscal trajectory and a very high violent-crime baseline dominate the tails.

Source-lean: moderate Anglophone on crime/climate/Bolsonaro-trial framing; macro from Brazilian/multilateral primaries (Agencia Brasil treated as partially-entangled, used only for uncontested figures).

economic.notes: An independent, credible central bank and sound banks, against a worsening debt trajectory and a high interest burden.

fiscal_state.notes: The key concern: a widening primary deficit, gross debt projected toward ~99% by 2030, doubted fiscal-framework credibility, and a high interest burden (Selic ~14.5%). (IMF Art IV 2025; Tesouro)

monetary_independence.notes: The Banco Central is independent (since 2021) and operating with full autonomy, holding very high real rates. (IMF; Banco Central)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The real is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: IPCA ~4.9%, above the 3% target, fought via a high Selic. (Banco Central Focus)

banking_stability.notes: Large, well-capitalised, Basel-aligned, with competent supervision and advanced digital infrastructure (Pix). (BCB)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A diversified agri/iron-ore/oil exporter (record exports), China-concentrated; near-term hit by 2025 US tariffs; no severe resource curse given the diversified economy and institutions. (OEC)

institutional.notes: Courts that defended democracy after a coup attempt, credible statistics, against a corruption legacy and slow courts.

rule_of_law.notes: WJP 78/143 (mid-tier); the Supreme Court defended democracy (convicting and barring Bolsonaro over the coup attempt), but with a corruption legacy and slow courts. (WJP 2025)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Mixed: pockets of technocratic strength (the central bank, IBGE, the foreign ministry) against uneven federal/state administration. (observed)

press_freedom.notes: RSF ~52nd, up sharply post-2022, but pressured by ownership concentration and structural violence against journalists. (RSF 2026)

political_social.notes: Resilient institutions amid deep polarization, against very high inequality and violent crime.

political_stability.notes: Deep Lula-Bolsonaro polarization (Bolsonaro convicted/barred) with a 2026 election ahead; institutions showed resilience after the Jan-8 attempt. (AS/COA)

civil_liberties.notes: Broadly protected, with uneven enforcement and surveillance/police-conduct concerns. (V-Dem)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful, durable routes (retirement, investor, real estate; citizenship after 4y), Portuguese-speaking; a new e-visa requirement a minor friction. (gov sources)

social_cohesion.notes: High inequality, very high violent crime, and PCC/CV gangs expanding. (InSight Crime)

demographics.notes: Median age ~35; fertility falling to ~1.44; population peaks ~2041, ageing faster than expected. (IBGE)

geopolitical.notes: A non-aligned regional leader with no external conflict.

alliance_reliability.notes: A non-aligned BRICS leader, pragmatic, with few alliances to lose and some US tariff friction. (observed)

conflict_involvement.notes: No external conflict; internal organized-crime violence. (observed)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Open-ish, no controls; some US individual designations tied to the Bolsonaro trial. (observed)

physical_practical.notes: A universal-health system and affordable private tier, against very high violent crime, weak logistics, and Amazon/climate exposure.

climate.notes: Amazon dieback/tipping-point stress plus Northeast drought plus 2024 record fires/floods. (IPCC AR6; ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: The universal SUS is strained (access/sanitation gaps) with an affordable private tier. (Commonwealth Fund)

infrastructure.notes: Uneven, with weak logistics and large sanitation gaps. (observed)

crime_safety.notes: High violent crime (homicide ~16/100k, a decade-low but partly a disappearances artifact); PCC/CV gangs. (InSight Crime)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against flood/drought exposure. (Munich Re)

summary: An independent, credible central bank, sound banks, courts that defended democracy after a coup attempt, and lawful residency routes. But the outcome distribution skews negative: a worsening public-debt trajectory with a high interest burden, deep political polarization, very high inequality and violent crime, weak logistics, and Amazon/climate exposure.
